_meta.description: Comprehensive soft-skills ontology for SOTA resume feedback. Covers leadership, collaboration, communication, problem-solving, adaptability, ownership, innovation, and anti-patterns (buzzwords).

score.type: Signal classification: LEADERSHIP_SIGNAL | OWNERSHIP_SIGNAL | COLLABORATION_SIGNAL | COMMUNICATION_SIGNAL | PROBLEM_SOLVING_SIGNAL | INNOVATION_SIGNAL | ADAPTABILITY_SIGNAL | GROWTH_SIGNAL | BUZZWORD | WEAK_SIGNAL | NEGATIVE_SIGNAL
score.bonusIfOutcomePresent: Float bonus added when a quantified result follows
score.bonusIfScopePresent: Float bonus added when org scope (e.g. cross-team, company-wide) is stated
score.bonusIfToolNamed: Float bonus when a specific methodology/tool is named

feedback.suggestion: Actionable rewrite advice
feedback.exampleStrong: Gold-standard phrasing example
feedback.impliesSeniority: Seniority levels this phrase typically signals
feedback.redFlag: True if phrase is a net-negative signal
feedback.preserveIf: Condition under which even a weak phrase is acceptable

relations.strengthens: Keys of phrases that compound positively with this one

feedback.suggestion: Strong signal. Sharpen it with a concrete outcome — e.g. 'reduced average ramp time from 6 weeks to 3', 'improved test coverage from 40% to 80%', or 'all mentees promoted within 12 months'.
feedback.exampleStrong: Mentored 4 junior engineers, reducing average onboarding time by 35% and contributing to 3 promotions within 18 months.

feedback.suggestion: State team size, tenure, and a tangible outcome. 'Led a team' without context is vague. Add: team size, reporting structure, and what the team shipped or improved.
feedback.exampleStrong: Led a team of 8 engineers across 3 time zones, shipping 2 major product features on time and reducing on-call incidents by 40%.

feedback.suggestion: Extremely strong signal at Staff/Principal/Director level. Add the planning horizon (quarterly, annual), stakeholder scope (exec-reviewed?), and at least one outcome from executing on the roadmap.
feedback.exampleStrong: Defined the 12-month technical roadmap for a 20-person platform team, aligning 4 product squads and reducing unplanned work by 30%.

feedback.suggestion: Differentiator at EM/Director level. Pair with a promotion count or team attrition stat to ground it.
feedback.exampleStrong: Conducted bi-annual performance reviews for 10 direct reports, resulting in 3 promotions and 0% voluntary attrition over 18 months.

feedback.suggestion: High-value signal for EMs and senior ICs who act as informal managers. Mention adoption (e.g. used by all 30 engineers in org) or promotion outcomes.
feedback.exampleStrong: Built the engineering career ladder adopted by 30 engineers across 4 teams, accelerating 6 promotions in the first cycle.

feedback.suggestion: Signals influence at scale. Specify who was aligned (exec, cross-org?) and what decision or outcome resulted. Vague alignment claims ring hollow.
feedback.exampleStrong: Drove alignment across engineering, product, and legal on a GDPR compliance initiative, unblocking a 6-month-delayed product launch.

feedback.suggestion: Strong for mid-to-senior roles. Name the audience level (VP, CTO, board), the topic, and what decision it influenced.
feedback.exampleStrong: Presented quarterly infrastructure reliability metrics to the CTO and board, leading to approval of a $2M observability investment.

feedback.suggestion: Add headcount numbers and outcome (e.g. time-to-fill, offer acceptance rate, team performance post-hire).
feedback.exampleStrong: Hired 12 engineers in 6 months, redesigning the technical interview to reduce time-to-offer by 40% and improving offer acceptance rate to 85%.

feedback.suggestion: Process facilitation alone is low signal. Elevate it by citing team velocity improvement, defect reduction, or delivery predictability gains.
feedback.exampleStrong: Facilitated agile ceremonies for a 10-person team, increasing sprint predictability from 60% to 90% over two quarters.
feedback.preserveIf: Acceptable standalone for roles where Agile delivery is a primary responsibility (Scrum Master, Delivery Manager).

feedback.suggestion: Name the specific functions (Product, Design, Legal, Finance) and the output of the collaboration. Generic cross-functional claims are common; specificity differentiates.
feedback.exampleStrong: Partnered with Product, Design, and Legal to launch a GDPR-compliant onboarding flow, cutting time-to-consent by 20%.

feedback.suggestion: Good signal for engineering quality culture. Add what it produced — defect rate reduction, knowledge transfer, faster feature delivery.
feedback.exampleStrong: Introduced pair programming sessions for critical modules, reducing post-release defects by 25% over one quarter.

feedback.suggestion: Expected at all engineering levels — mention volume or process impact to stand out. 'Reviewed PRs' alone is table stakes.
feedback.exampleStrong: Established code review standards for a 15-person team, reducing PR cycle time from 3 days to under 4 hours.
feedback.preserveIf: Acceptable on junior resumes where it signals engagement with team process.

feedback.suggestion: Valuable for senior/client-facing roles. Name the partner type and the commercial or technical outcome.
feedback.exampleStrong: Partnered with AWS Professional Services to architect a multi-region failover solution, achieving 99.99% uptime SLA.

feedback.suggestion: Underrated signal for senior ICs and EMs. Add scope (team-wide? org-wide?) and impact (reduced support tickets, improved onboarding time).
feedback.exampleStrong: Authored 30+ runbooks and postmortem templates adopted across 6 teams, reducing mean time to resolve incidents by 45%.

feedback.suggestion: High-value signal at Staff/Principal. Mention the number of RFCs, adoption rate, or the system decisions they influenced.
feedback.exampleStrong: Authored 12 RFCs over 18 months, establishing platform standards adopted by all 40 engineers and replacing 3 competing legacy patterns.

feedback.suggestion: Excellent differentiator. Name the event, audience size, and topic. External conferences carry more weight than internal talks.
feedback.exampleStrong: Keynoted QCon London 2025 on distributed tracing at scale (800+ attendees), leading to 3 enterprise partnership inquiries.

feedback.suggestion: Name the publication, topic, and reach (views, shares, or backlinks). Viral content is far more impressive than volume alone.
feedback.exampleStrong: Published 8 articles on Stripe's engineering blog reaching 50K+ readers, two of which trended on Hacker News.

feedback.suggestion: Add participant count, recurrence, and tangible output from the workshop (decision made, prototype built, process adopted).
feedback.exampleStrong: Designed and facilitated quarterly architecture workshops for 25 engineers, producing 6 ADRs that unified platform standards.

feedback.suggestion: Frame with business impact: what was the system impact, how long did diagnosis take, and what was the outcome?
feedback.exampleStrong: Diagnosed a memory leak causing 3 daily pod crashes in production; root cause was unbounded gRPC connection pooling. Fix deployed in 4 hours, restoring 99.9% uptime.

feedback.suggestion: High signal for reliability-focused roles. Mention action items completed, recurrence reduction, or cultural change driven.
feedback.exampleStrong: Led blameless postmortem process for 15+ incidents, driving 8 systemic fixes that reduced P1 recurrence rate by 60% in 6 months.

feedback.suggestion: Powerful signal at Staff+ and EM level. Ground it in a specific scenario: what was ambiguous, how was it framed, and what decision or output resulted.
feedback.exampleStrong: Decomposed a vague exec directive ('improve reliability') into a prioritized 6-month OKR set, gaining eng and PM sign-off in one planning cycle.

feedback.suggestion: Strong when concrete. Name the metric, analysis type, and decision it drove. 'Data-driven' alone is becoming a buzzword — specificity rescues it.
feedback.exampleStrong: Ran 12 A/B experiments using Statsig, uncovering a checkout friction point that, once fixed, improved conversion by 14%.

feedback.suggestion: Ownership without outcomes feels hollow. Add: what you owned, how long, and the scale (traffic, users, revenue impact).
feedback.exampleStrong: Sole owner of the payments service serving 2M daily transactions — designed, shipped, and maintained with 99.95% uptime over 2 years.

feedback.suggestion: Shipping is table stakes. Elevate with: timeline, scale, and business impact. What changed because you shipped it?
feedback.exampleStrong: Shipped real-time notification system in 6 weeks (solo), supporting 500K users at launch and driving a 22% increase in DAU.

feedback.suggestion: Excellent signal for SRE, Platform, and senior backend roles. Always pair with before/after numbers.
feedback.exampleStrong: Reduced on-call pages from 40/week to 3/week by eliminating toil through automated remediation runbooks and SLO-based alerting.

feedback.suggestion: Always quote the dollar (or %) saving and the baseline. 'Reduced costs' without a number is near-useless.
feedback.exampleStrong: Rightsized 200+ EC2 instances and migrated cold data to S3 Glacier, saving $480K/year (38% of infra budget).

feedback.suggestion: Quantify the deal value, savings, or improved terms. Pure negotiation without outcome is weak.
feedback.exampleStrong: Renegotiated Snowflake enterprise contract, securing a 30% price reduction and improved SLA from 99.5% to 99.9% uptime.

feedback.suggestion: Name the technology, the adoption scope, and the measured improvement. Beware: introducing tech that was later abandoned is neutral or negative.
feedback.exampleStrong: Championed adoption of OpenTelemetry across 12 services, replacing 3 proprietary monitoring vendors and saving $120K/year.

feedback.suggestion: POCs without outcomes are dead ends on a resume. Always state whether it was productionised, how it influenced a decision, or what it validated.
feedback.exampleStrong: Built a LangChain POC for automated code review in 2 weeks; validated at 85% precision, leading to a funded Q3 productionisation project.

feedback.suggestion: Standards without adoption are useless. State who adopted them (N teams, N engineers) and the quality impact.
feedback.exampleStrong: Defined API versioning standards adopted by all 8 backend teams, reducing breaking changes from 12/quarter to 1.

feedback.suggestion: Pivots need a before-state, trigger, action, and after-state to be credible. What changed and how did you lead through it?
feedback.exampleStrong: Pivoted team from a monolith rebuild to a service-by-service extraction after a strategic product shift, delivering first service in 4 weeks with no team attrition.

feedback.suggestion: Leadership through adversity is compelling. Be specific about the challenge, the actions you took, and the outcome (retention, delivery, morale scores).
feedback.exampleStrong: Maintained 100% team retention and on-time delivery of 2 major features during a 40% company-wide layoff round, through proactive communication and reprioritisation.

feedback.suggestion: Learning agility is underrated but only credible with a timeline and a shipped outcome. 'Quickly learned Rust' is fine; 'learned Rust and shipped a latency-critical service in 3 months' is great.
feedback.exampleStrong: Transitioned from mobile to backend in 4 months, shipping a Go microservice handling 50K RPS within my first quarter on the platform team.

feedback.suggestion: Modest signal unless paired with a visible change. What did you do differently as a result of the feedback?
feedback.exampleStrong: After 360 feedback flagged communication gaps, restructured weekly team updates and increased stakeholder satisfaction scores by 30% in next survey.

feedback.suggestion: Name the project, your role (maintainer vs contributor), and scale (stars, downloads, merged PRs). Maintainer of a 5K-star project is elite signal.
feedback.exampleStrong: Core maintainer of an open-source observability toolkit (8.2K GitHub stars, 1M+ monthly downloads) used by teams at Shopify and Cloudflare.

match: delivered on time and within budget

feedback.suggestion: Add stakes: how complex was the project, how many people were involved, what was at risk? On-time delivery of a solo task is minimal signal.
feedback.exampleStrong: Delivered a 6-month platform migration on time and $80K under budget, coordinating 3 engineering teams and zero production incidents during cutover.

feedback.suggestion: Quantify the coverage delta (% before and after) and tie to a reliability or deployment frequency improvement.
feedback.exampleStrong: Raised unit test coverage from 18% to 82% over one quarter, reducing production regressions by 65% and enabling weekly deployments.

feedback.suggestion: DORA metrics are the gold standard here. Mention deploy frequency, lead time, change failure rate, or MTTR.
feedback.exampleStrong: Rebuilt CI pipeline using Buildkite, cutting build time from 28 minutes to 4 and increasing deploy frequency from weekly to 15+ per day.

match: scaled from X to Y users
scaled from X to Y requests

feedback.suggestion: Scaling claims need hard numbers — orders of magnitude, peak RPS, user count, or data volume. Vague scaling claims are ignored.
feedback.exampleStrong: Scaled search infrastructure from 10K to 2M QPS over 8 months using Elasticsearch sharding optimisation and query caching, maintaining p99 < 120ms.

feedback.suggestion: Remove immediately. This is universally expected and reads as filler. Replace with a specific cross-team collaboration that had a measurable outcome.
feedback.exampleStrong: N/A — delete this phrase entirely.

feedback.suggestion: Remove. Every candidate claims this. Show it through output: what did your dedication actually produce?
feedback.exampleStrong: N/A — delete this phrase entirely.

feedback.suggestion: Remove — these adjectives are self-reported and unverifiable. Show initiative through a specific example of taking ownership without being asked.
feedback.exampleStrong: N/A — delete this phrase entirely.

feedback.suggestion: Weak when stated directly. Prove it: 'Picked up Rust in 8 weeks and shipped a latency-critical service' is far stronger than claiming you learn fast.
feedback.exampleStrong: N/A — replace with evidence: a new skill acquired and a shipped outcome.
feedback.preserveIf: Acceptable only for career changers or recent grads where it contextualises a non-traditional background — but still only with supporting evidence.

feedback.suggestion: Overused self-descriptor. If accuracy matters in your role, show it through: defect rates, audit pass rates, or quality-related outcomes.
feedback.exampleStrong: N/A — replace with: 'Achieved 0 critical defects across 3 production releases by introducing automated regression suite.'

feedback.suggestion: Critical issue — passive language hides your actual impact. Rewrite with an active verb that reflects YOUR specific contribution.
feedback.exampleStrong: Instead of 'helped with the migration', write: 'Designed the data model and wrote the migration scripts for a 200-table Postgres-to-Aurora migration.'
feedback.preserveIf: Acceptable ONLY if you were genuinely a minor contributor and want to be honest — but consider whether this bullet adds any value at all.

feedback.suggestion: Describes a job description, not an achievement. Replace with an action verb and a result. Responsibility ≠ impact.
feedback.exampleStrong: Instead of 'Responsible for backend API', write: 'Designed and maintained the core REST API serving 4M daily requests at p95 < 50ms.'

feedback.suggestion: Everyone claims this. Show it: 'Authored RFC that aligned 5 teams' or 'Keynoted internal conference of 300 engineers' demonstrates communication without stating it.
feedback.exampleStrong: N/A — delete and show communication through specific high-impact written or verbal acts.

feedback.suggestion: Delete. This reads as filler and signals a lack of strong accomplishments. Hiring teams look for impact, not likability claims.
feedback.exampleStrong: N/A — delete entirely.

feedback.suggestion: Never self-describe as a 'thought leader' or 'visionary'. These are earned designations. Replace with the strategic outcome you produced.
feedback.exampleStrong: N/A — delete and replace with: 'Defined 3-year platform vision adopted by CTO and board, reallocating $4M in roadmap investment.'

feedback.suggestion: Remove from professional summary. Passion is demonstrated through OSS contributions, side projects, publications, and conference talks — not stated.
feedback.exampleStrong: N/A — replace with a proof of passion: a notable side project, open-source contribution, or published work.
feedback.preserveIf: Marginally acceptable in objective/summary for junior candidates on their first 1-2 roles where they lack sufficient professional evidence.

feedback.suggestion: Corporate jargon is an active negative signal — it reads as either lazy or inexperienced. Replace with plain language describing the actual action and outcome.
feedback.exampleStrong: N/A — delete entirely and rewrite in plain language.
